title: My changing sleep is not my failure
subtitle: An affirmation for the guilt and frustration of sleep that has changed
excerpt: For the point where poor sleep has started to feel like a personal failing. The tiredness is compounded by shame. A statement for separating the experience from the self-judgement.

practice.whenToUse: When poor sleep has become a source of shame or self-blame on top of the tiredness itself

content.text: Sleep changes across life stages, hormonal shifts, and stress. When sleep deteriorates, there is often a layer of self-blame added to the tiredness: something is wrong with me, I should be better at this, other people sleep fine. The self-blame adds weight without adding sleep.
Say this three times, hand on chest if that helps:
My changing sleep is not my failure.
Then ask: what is one thing I can do differently tonight, not to force sleep but to make the conditions a little better? One small thing. Start there.
